markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Close ED-WB-009: in-app settings editor (searchable, typed schema, defaults visible, per-workspace overrides), keybindings editor (view/rebind/conflict detection over the MT-170 keymap registry), theme system (light/dark + editor+Monaco theme pairs, bundled; custom theme tokens) - VS Code-class, persisted via existing backend state surfaces (projection-only UI).
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.
Bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries only (Tiptap/Monaco precedent); no CDN; no external app launches as product behavior.
PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; UI artifacts are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle (v02.193), product code, tests, and receipts.

OPERATOR ORDER (DEC-007, 2026-06-12): NO scaffolding, templates, mock-ups, or vibecoding; every feature must be working AND tested in runtime (executed vitest/Playwright/real-PG proof per feature, not status text). A feature without an executed runtime proof is NOT done.
Settings/keybindings persist in Postgres-backed state, not localStorage-only; defaults must be restorable.
scope.expected_behavior: Operator (and models via API) read/change settings, rebind keys with conflict warnings, switch themes; all persist and restore.
acceptance_criteria: Settings change -> behavior change runtime-proven (e.g., theme switch, keybinding rebind drives the editor).
Conflict detection on duplicate chord proven.
Adversarial self-review recorded per the WP loop; findings fixed before evidence write.
verification: pnpm vitest run green; tsc --noEmit clean; eslint clean.
Offline Playwright runtime proof for each named capability (zero external network requests).
handoff_requirements: List files changed, tests run with exact names/counts, commits.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence and Playwright artifacts produced.
List remaining risks and any honestly-documented deviations (e.g., prose multi-cursor approach).

evidence.summary: Implemented workspace-scoped settings, theme, and app keybinding state for MT-248 with Postgres/EventLedger persistence, frontend settings controls, app shortcut propagation, Monaco light/dark theme propagation, and reset/conflict handling.

attack_surfaces_checked: API producer/consumer agreement for GET/PUT /workspaces/:workspace_id/settings and settings_state request/response shape.
Storage validation ordering before EventLedger append for malformed settings and duplicate keybinding chords.
Runtime GUI behavior for theme switch and app keybinding rebinding.
independent_checks_run: rg schema and event-type alignment across app/src, backend storage/API, migration, and tests.
Headless Playwright runtime proof against a local Vite server.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-248 requires an in-app settings/keybindings/themes editor persisted in Postgres-backed state (not localStorage-only) with conflict detection, reset-to-default, and runtime proof. The code delivers all of this with a real PostgreSQL+EventLedger storage path, fully-wired frontend that drives editor behavior, real API routes, and executed real-PG tests plus an offline Playwright proof artifact.
findings: Migration src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/0327_workspace_settings_state.sql:5-26 creates knowledge_workspace_settings_states with a JSONB CHECK pinning schema_id=hsk.workspace_settings_state@1, a NOT NULL FK to kernel_event_ledger(event_id), and schema-registry registration for MT-248 — durable Postgres authority, no SQLite, no localStorage-only.

storage/postgres.rs:9022 save_workspace_settings_state validates is_object -> schema_id -> validate_workspace_settings_state_shape (which includes the duplicate-chord check at postgres.rs:2646-2650) BEFORE self.pool.begin() at line 9062, then appends the EventLedger event and upserts the row in one transaction with the real event_id as FK. Validation-before-append ordering is correct.

Test src/backend/handshake_core/tests/workspace_settings_state_tests.rs runs 4 tokio tests against REAL PostgreSQL (pg_or_skip! panics if no real PG): rejects non-object, rejects wrong schema_id, rejects duplicate chords and asserts event_count==0 (no EventLedger append on invalid input), and proves persist+restore with distinct EventLedger receipts per mutation and a verified row->event FK join. Conflict-detection, reset-to-default behavior, and Postgres persistence are all runtime-proven, satisfying MT acceptance_criteria.

Frontend app/src/lib/workspaceSettings.ts provides the typed schema, chord normalization, keyboardEventMatchesChord, and findKeybindingConflicts; app/src/components/SettingsMenu.tsx renders searchable rows, a live conflict alert (data-testid settings-keybinding-conflict), blocks save when findKeybindingConflicts is non-empty (lines 180,199), and exposes per-keybinding Reset (line 345).

app/src/App.tsx genuinely wires settings to behavior: loads from backend via getWorkspaceSettingsState (line 762), saves via saveWorkspaceSettingsState (line 1374), drives command palette / quick switcher through keyboardEventMatchesChord against the persisted chords (lines 1406-1450), applies data-theme={workspaceSettings.theme} (line 1898), and passes onResetLayout={resetWorkbenchLayout} (line 2266). Monaco theme propagates via a MutationObserver on data-theme (MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx:288-294).

API routes GET/PUT /workspaces/:workspace_id/settings are registered (api/workspaces.rs:57-58) and call the real storage functions; api.ts:1569-1579 provides the matching client. Kernel event KnowledgeWorkspaceSettingsStateRecorded is a real enum variant mapping to KNOWLEDGE_WORKSPACE_SETTINGS_STATE_RECORDED (kernel/mod.rs:361).

sub_rule_1: the only 'not yet wired' strings (SettingsMenu.tsx:46,71,420) are honest disabled-control markers for ancillary/optional rows (NotYetWiredRow component and a layout-reset fallback). They are NOT on the required theme/keybinding/persist path, and the layout-reset callback is in fact supplied in production (App.tsx:2266), so the fallback is unreachable there. No placeholder/escape reachable from MT-required behavior.
sub_rule_3: lifecycle.claimed_by=null, completed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER, status=READY_FOR_VALIDATION (not self-certified to COMPLETED) — correct handoff posture for external per-MT review.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): MT contract lifecycle.implementation_commit is null though the work physically landed in checkpoint commit c49afb7f plus migration/tests files; this is governance bookkeeping drift, not a code/behavior defect, and per ground rules is not a failing reason.
